<!-- GENERATED by scripts/build-codex.mjs from plugins/relay/skills/report/ — do not hand-edit; re-run the generator. -->

# report — turn a new intent into the right GitHub object

Create the smallest durable object that matches what the human wants, then verify that GitHub shows the intended responsibility.

## Router

Ask in order:

1. **Is there a clear completion condition?** No -> Discussion.
2. **Is one person accountable for advancing or deciding it?** No -> Discussion.
3. **Is there an exact reviewable diff?** Yes -> pull request; no -> Issue.

**Upstream nursery (ADR-098).** Questions 1 and 2 collapse to one practical boundary: can you state an owner and a completion rule yet? Not yet — still diverging, unclear whether or what to do — stays a Discussion: Ideas for open-ended exploration, Q&A when there's a specific question with an accepted-answer completion condition. Nobody owing work there is a *feature* — a Discussion is the nursery where convergence happens before anything is tracked as work, matching GitHub's own stated purpose for the object type (developing and prioritizing ideas before an Issue exists). Once an owner and a completion rule can be stated, open the Issue and link back to the originating Discussion — GitHub's own create-issue-from-discussion affordance preserves that lineage natively. An ask that's born crisp may open the Issue directly; the two-stage path is never mandatory ceremony. **The smell this kills:** an Issue where nobody can say what "done" looks like — that should have been (or stayed) a Discussion.

Use these refinements:

- pure FYI (a broadcast, no reply or receipt expected) -> Announcement Discussion explicitly labeled `fyi`, naming at least one explicit `@recipient` in the title or body, no assignment or ACK marker; every report tags a recipient, FYI included — an untagged FYI is a broadcast into the void, and `relay-digest`'s `mentioned-in-prose` notice is what guarantees a tagged FYI still reaches that person, even though FYI itself carries no obligation;
- FYI closure: the initiator closes every FYI — one closure-owner class, same as any other Discussion. Each recipient's seen-signal is `👀` (a comment is welcome when a recipient has something to say, but is never required); the initiator checks the reaction list to see who has seen it, then closes as ordinary housekeeping, no resolution comment needed. See ADR-096;
- authoritative external source update -> `fyi`-labeled Announcement with source URL and immutable revision, commit, or content hash; update any reusable brief through a separate cited PR;
- **any other Announcement is receipt-default (ADR-097).** An Announcement Discussion — no `fyi` label — naming one or more `@recipient`s is receipt-bearing by construction: EVERY named account owes its own awareness receipt, completed only by that account's own `👀`, not a single designated recipient. This records "I saw this notice," never proof of comprehension, acceptance, or external work. The `[ACK]` title prefix or an `ack-required` label still work as an explicit marker, but neither is required any more — naming someone in a non-Q&A, non-`fyi` Discussion IS the receipt trigger. Naming several people means each of them separately owes a receipt; if you want a bystander mention with no obligation attached, that belongs on an `fyi`-labeled Announcement instead. The author's own self-mention is excluded — naming yourself in your own announcement never creates a receipt for you, and never blocks the close-nudge. A `@org/team-name` path is recognized and never counted as a recipient. Once every named recipient has reacted, the announcement's author owes closing it — `relay-digest` surfaces this as a SETTLE `close-announcement` obligation (reason `receipts-collected`), symmetric with Q&A's answered -> close, so nobody has to manually poll the reaction list. Name real, reachable individual accounts: a typo, a nonexistent handle, or a bare `@org` mention (not distinguishable from a user login) is silently counted as a recipient and will block the close-nudge forever if it never reacts, with no self-report;
- **content, not just receipt, wanted** -> if what you actually want back is response CONTENT — an opinion, a decision, information — not just proof that someone looked, it is not an ACK. Open it as a Q&A Discussion whose question states exactly what they should answer; their obligation is an answer you accept, not a reaction;
- answerable question -> Q&A Discussion;
- verifiable work -> Issue with one assignee, observable completion criteria, and the evidence the assignee must return;
- one authorized decision -> Decision Issue with that person as the single assignee; reversing or materially amending a previously agreed decision routes the same way — a Decision Issue assigned to the counterpart whose authored `Outcome:` is the consent record — and an accompanying Announcement may inform but never substitutes, since its `👀` (if any) still only ever attests awareness, not agreement (ADR-099);
- exact material that must be read and judged -> pull request with the required reviewer and a current-revision verdict; the author owns merge by default, or one PR assignee explicitly names a different merger;
- exact change -> the same pull request path; merge, not manual close, is the accepted change's effective point.

Split independently completable asks into linked objects. Three phrases that sound similar are different events: “I saw this notice” uses ACK; “review this exact material” uses a requested PR verdict; “install, restart, run, or verify this” uses an assigned Issue with evidence. Never let `👀` stand in for a verdict, command output, version, test, or changed external state. Do not let a partial child object close a broader parent.

**Entry-owner discipline.** Any ask that expects a response must land in a natively-owned shape: an assigned Issue, a requested-reviewer PR, or a receipt-default Announcement naming its recipient(s). A prose `@mention` typed directly into a title, body, or comment is never itself an entry point — it reaches only `relay-digest`'s non-binding notices tier, not anyone's obligations, so an ask that only ever exists as prose can go unanswered indefinitely. If it doesn't fit one of the three owned shapes, it is FYI by definition — route it there instead of leaving it as an unowned mention and hoping. The PR route requires naming a requested reviewer at creation, not leaving it to be added later — and that reviewer must be an individual, never a team: v1 digest (`relay-digest`) cannot route a team review request to anyone, so a team-requested review is invisible to every member's obligations. This discipline governs traffic created *through* `report`; organic traffic — someone opening a GitHub Issue or Discussion directly — is nudged toward the same explicit-recipient rule by the repository entry-point templates `relay-launch` installs and audits (ADR-094).

An FYI completes when the Announcement and its provenance read back; it creates no response or settlement obligation. A receipt-default Announcement completes per-recipient — each named account's own `👀` clears only their own receipt — and offers its author a close-nudge once every recipient has reacted (ADR-097). A requested reviewer completes only their review round; approval hands the PR to its author or named merger, and merge auto-closes the accepted change. Manually closing it unmerged records abandonment. A task Issue completes only when its stated evidence exists and the result is settled, not when a progress comment appears.

## Process

1. Resolve the current repository and authenticated viewer. If the user supplied an existing object URL, inspect and resume it instead of creating anything.
2. Distill title, body, object type, completion condition, recipient/owner/reviewer, required verdict or evidence, and provenance links. State whether the traffic is FYI, awareness ACK, action, decision, or exact review. If it mixes awareness, review, and execution, show the linked objects separately.
3. **Author sign-off.** Show the exact title and body that will be posted, verbatim — this speaks in the user's voice on GitHub — and ask: "Is this what you mean?" Post only after they confirm; a rewrite goes through the same gate. Show the follow-up mutations (assignment, review request, labels, links) alongside it. Wait for approval.
4. Create the object, then apply assignment, review request, labels/category, and links as separate observable steps.
5. Read the object back. Verify URL, type/category, assignee or requested reviewer, current revision, and requested obligation.

## Partial failure

If creation succeeds but a later mutation fails, return the created URL plus the exact missing steps. Retry from that URL. Never create a duplicate to obtain a clean run.

## Completion

Done means the object exists and its intended responsibility is visible in GitHub. A prose mention alone never creates work; silent assignment or reviewer failure blocks completion.

## Boundaries

`report` opens. `relay-reply` responds. `relay-settle` declares the whole object finished or effective. Durable cross-context synthesis belongs to `relay-brief`.

## Communication style

- Explain in the user's language with simple, direct wording.
- Lead with the result; put technical details after it.
